title: Bespoked Launches SRAM 2026 Inclusivity Scholarship Applications
content:
Bespoked and SRAM are proud to announce the return of the Inclusivity Scholarship for a fourth consecutive year, continuing their shared commitment to helping create a cycling industry that is more welcoming, representative, and accessible…

Following a landmark year for Bespoked Dresden, including a sold-out London show that brought together builders, brands, and visitors from across the world, the scholarship returns for 2026 with a continued focus on creating opportunities for talented framebuilders.

The scholarship supports people who feel they are not currently represented fairly within cycling and framebuilding, offering an opportunity to exhibit at Bespoked Dresden, taking place 11–13 September 2026 at Dresden International Airport.

Over the past three years, the programme has helped scholarship recipients share their work with an international audience, build industry connections, and become part of a growing community shaping the future of handmade cycling.

SRAM 2026 Inclusivity Scholarship Details

Recipients of the scholarship will receive:

  • Exhibition space and staging at Bespoked Dresden 2026.
  • SRAM components to support the creation of a scholarship show build.
  • Financial support toward accommodation costs.
  • Dedicated promotional coverage across Bespoked and SRAM channels to help tell recipients’ stories and showcase their work.
  • Support and mentorship from experienced industry professionals in the lead-up to the event.

Applications open: Monday, 1st June
Applications close: Thursday, 25th June
Successful applicants notified: Wednesday, 1st July

Apply at Bespoked!

The post Bespoked Launches SRAM 2026 Inclusivity Scholarship Applications appeared first on The Radavist.
author: John Watson
